content.text:
This is a todo list app where I have two models, a todo list and a todo items. Also, I have the controllers and the views. The goal now is to use hotwire so that the web app is more real time, we could use turbo frames and/or turbo streams.
I attached a very simple mock for the ui I did it on my own. Use that mock to improve the ui and also use turbo frames and/or turbo streams.
The idea is to have the todo list listed on the left with the possibility of editing and deleting each todo list. Also, we should be able to add a todo list. Similar for the todo items, they should be listed on the right and we should have the possibility of editing and deleting todo items. Apart from that we should have a checkbox for mark a ticket as completed. Use tailwind to improve the ui. Also, fell free to improve the mockup.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. content.text:
Perfect! I've gathered all the requested information. Here's a comprehensive report of the TodoList and TodoItem feature:

## Model Associations & Validations

**TodoList Model** (`app/models/todo_list.rb`):
```ruby
class TodoList < ApplicationRecord
  has_many :todo_items, dependent: :destroy
  validates :name, presence: true
end
```
- **Association**: One-to-many relationship with TodoItem; destroys dependent items when a list is deleted
- **Validation**: `name` field is required (presence validation)

**TodoItem Model** (`app/models/todo_item.rb`):
```ruby
class TodoItem < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :todo_list
  validates :title, presence: true
  validates :complete, inclusion: { in: [true, false] }
end
```
- **Association**: Belongs to TodoList (foreign key: `todo_list_id`)
- **Validations**: 
  - `title` is required
  - `complete` must be a boolean (true or false)

---

## Controller Actions & Responses

**TodoListsController** (`app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`):
- **index** (GET `/todolists`): Fetches all todo lists; responds with HTML
- **new** (GET `/todolists/new`): Builds a new TodoList; responds with HTML
- **create** (POST `/todolists`): Creates a new TodoList; redirects to index on success with notice, or re-renders `new` with 422 status on failure

**TodoItemsController** (`app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`):
Uses nested routes with before_action callbacks:
- **set_todo_list**: Finds the parent TodoList by `params[:todo_list_id]`
- **set_todo_item**: Finds the TodoItem scoped to the parent list

Actions:
- **index** (GET `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items`): Lists all items for a list
- **show** (GET `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/:id`): Shows a single item
- **new** (GET `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/new`): Builds a new item
- **edit** (GET `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/:id/edit`): Prepares item for editing
- **create** (POST `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items`): Creates item; redirects to index with notice or re-renders `new` with 422 on failure
- **update** (PATCH/PUT `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/:id`): Updates item; redirects to index with notice or re-renders `edit` with 422 on failure
- **destroy** (DELETE `/todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/:id`): Deletes item; redirects to index with notice

All responses are HTML-based (no JSON or turbo_stream responses defined).

---

## Routes Definition

```ruby
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  namespace :api do
    resources :todo_lists, only: %i[index], path: :todolists
  end

  resources :todo_lists, only: %i[index new create], path: :todolists do
    resources :todo_items, path: :todoitems
  end
end
```

**Routing Summary**:
- **TodoLists** (non-namespaced):
  - `/todolists` → index, new, create
  - Nested route: `/todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems`
  
- **TodoItems** (nested under TodoLists):
  - Full CRUD actions (index, show, new, edit, create, update, destroy)
  - Path prefix: `/todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems`

- **API namespace** (read-only):
  - `/api/todolists` → index only (TodoListsController not specified for API)

---

## Database Schema

**todo_lists table**:
```
t.string "name", null: false
```
- `id` (implicit primary key)
- `name` (required string)

**todo_items table**:
```
t.string "title", null: false
t.boolean "complete", default: false, null: false
t.integer "todo_list_id", null: false
t.datetime "created_at", null: false
t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
```
- `id` (implicit primary key)
- `title` (required string)
- `complete` (required boolean, defaults to false)
- `todo_list_id` (required integer, foreign key to todo_lists)
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (Rails timestamps)

**Indexes & Constraints**:
- Index on `todo_items.todo_list_id`
- Foreign key constraint: `todo_items.todo_list_id` → `todo_lists.id`

file.content:
class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list
  before_action :set_todo_item, only: %i[ show edit update destroy ]

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items
  def index
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def show
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items 
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully created.'
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def update
    if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully updated.'
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def destroy
    @todo_item.destroy!
    redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully destroyed.'
  end

  private
    # Use callbacks to share common setup or constraints between actions.
    def set_todo_list
      @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
    end

    # Use callbacks to share common setup or constraints between actions.
    def set_todo_item
      @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    end

    # Only allow a list of trusted parameters through.
    def todo_item_params
      params.require(:todo_item).permit(:title, :complete)
    end
end

file.content:
class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all

    respond_to :html
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new

    respond_to :html
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)
    if @todo_list.save
      redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully created.'
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

content.text:
I now fully understand the codebase. Here's what I found:

- **Turbo + Stimulus** are already installed (importmap-based, Rails 7.0.4). **Tailwind is not.**
- `TodoList` only has `index/new/create` routes — **no edit, update, destroy, or show**.
- `TodoItem` has full CRUD nested under lists, but all responses are HTML redirects (no turbo_stream).
- Views are scaffold defaults with inline styles. RSpec is set up.

The plan needs to: install Tailwind, add the missing TodoList routes/actions, convert controllers to respond with Turbo Streams, and rebuild the views into the two-pane layout from your mockup. Let me clarify a couple of decisions that shape the build.

questions.question: How should Tailwind CSS be installed? This affects how you run the app in dev.

options.label: tailwindcss-rails gem (Recommended)
options.description: Standalone Tailwind, no Node needed. Integrates with the asset pipeline and a real build step. Dev runs via bin/dev (Procfile watches CSS). Production-grade and standard for Rails 7 importmap apps.

options.label: Tailwind CDN script
options.description: Add one <script> tag to the layout. Zero build setup, app still runs with bin/puma. Quick for a demo but not recommended for production (no purging, slower).

questions.question: How should adding/editing lists and items behave in the UI?

options.label: Inline on one page (Recommended)
options.description: Everything stays on the single two-pane page. 'Add' reveals an inline form; clicking edit swaps a row into an inline edit form via Turbo Frames. Closest to a real-time SPA feel and the mockup.

options.label: Separate new/edit pages
options.description: Keep navigating to dedicated new/edit pages, just styled with Tailwind. Simpler but less 'real-time'.

questions.question: Include the 'Check all' control from the mockup?

options.label: Yes, include it
options.description: Add a 'Check all' button above items that marks every item in the selected list complete via a Turbo Stream update.

options.label: Skip it for now
options.description: Only per-item completion checkboxes. Can be added later.

input.content:
# Hotwire + Tailwind Todo List App

## Context

The app is a Rails 7.0.4 Todo app (importmap-based) with `TodoList` and `TodoItem` models. Turbo + Stimulus are already installed, but the UI is unstyled scaffold output and every action does a full-page redirect. Tailwind is not installed, and `TodoList` is missing `show/edit/update/destroy` routes.

The goal: a single-page, two-pane app matching the user's mockup — **Todo Lists on the left, Todo Items (of the selected list) on the right** — where create/edit/delete/complete all happen inline without full page reloads, using **Turbo Frames + Turbo Streams** and styled with **Tailwind**.

Decisions confirmed with the user:
- Install Tailwind via the **`tailwindcss-rails` gem** (run app with `bin/dev`).
- **Inline** add/edit on the single page (Turbo Frames swap rows into forms).
- Include the **"Check all"** control (marks all items in a list complete).

## Implementation

### 1. Install Tailwind
- Add `gem "tailwindcss-rails"` to the `Gemfile`, `bundle install`, then run `bin/rails tailwindcss:install`.
- This generates `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css`, a `Procfile.dev`, `bin/dev`, and updates the layout to `<%= stylesheet_link_tag "tailwind", "data-turbo-track": "reload" %>`. Verify `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` picks up the Tailwind stylesheet.

### 2. Routes — `config/routes.rb`
Promote `todo_lists` to full CRUD and add a `check_all` collection route on items. Leave the `api` namespace untouched.
```ruby
resources :todo_lists, path: :todolists do
  resources :todo_items, path: :todoitems do
    patch :check_all, on: :collection
  end
end
root "todo_lists#index"
```

### 3. Controllers

**`app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`** — add `show`, `edit`, `update`, `destroy` and a `set_todo_list` before_action.
- `index`: `@todo_lists = TodoList.all`; set `@todo_list = @todo_lists.first` to seed the right pane, and `@new_todo_list = TodoList.new` for the add form.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: `respond_to` with `format.turbo_stream` (success) and `format.html` fallback; re-render the form partial with `status: :unprocessable_entity` on validation failure.

**`app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`** — add Turbo Stream responses and `check_all`.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: respond with `format.turbo_stream`.
- The per-item completion checkbox reuses `update` (submits `complete`).
- `check_all`: `@todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)` then re-render the items list via Turbo Stream.

### 4. Views — two-pane layout

**`todo_lists/index.html.erb`**: page header "Todo List App"; responsive 2-column grid (`grid md:grid-cols-2`). Left = lists pane; right = `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` rendering the selected list's items (or a "Select a list" placeholder when none).

**Left pane (lists):**
- `todo_lists/_todo_list.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_list)`; shows the name as a link that targets the `"todo_items_pane"` frame (`data: { turbo_frame: "todo_items_pane" }` → `todo_list_todo_items_path`), plus edit (pencil) and delete (✕) buttons. Container div `id="todo_lists"`.
- `todo_lists/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled, reused for the inline "Add todo list" form (persistent, `id="new_todo_list_form"`) and inline edit (rendered inside `edit.html.erb` wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(@todo_list)`).

**Right pane (items):**
- `todo_items/_pane.html.erb` (NEW shared partial): "Todo Items" header, "Check all" button (PATCH to `check_all_todo_list_todo_items_path`), inline "Add todo item" form, and a container `id="todo_items"` listing items.
- `todo_items/index.html.erb`: wraps `render "pane"` in `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` so list-row clicks lazy-load it; the lists index right pane renders the same partial — single source of truth.
- `todo_items/_todo_item.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_item)`; a completion checkbox in a mini `form_with` that auto-submits (`onchange: "this.form.requestSubmit()"`), the title (strikethrough when `complete`), edit + delete buttons.
- `todo_items/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled inline form (used for add + edit, like the lists form).

### 5. Turbo Stream templates
Add `*.turbo_stream.erb` for both controllers:
- Lists: `create` (prepend row to `#todo_lists` + reset `#new_todo_list_form`), `update` (replace `dom_id(list)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(list)`).
- Items: `create` (append to `#todo_items` + reset add-item form), `update` (replace `dom_id(item)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(item)`), `check_all` (replace `#todo_items` with the re-rendered list).

### 6. Tests — `spec/`
The current specs are mostly scaffold stubs; view specs reference markup being replaced and must be updated.
- Add model specs: `spec/models/todo_list_spec.rb`, `spec/models/todo_item_spec.rb` (validations, association, `complete` default).
- Fill in `spec/requests/todo_items_spec.rb` (real `valid_attributes`/`invalid_attributes`, assert turbo_stream responses for create/update/destroy + check_all). Add `spec/requests/todo_lists_spec.rb` covering the new actions.
- Update/replace the `spec/views/todo_items/*` specs to match the new partials, or convert to request-level coverage so the suite stays green.

## Files
- Modify: `Gemfile`, `config/routes.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`, `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb`, `app/views/todo_lists/*`, `app/views/todo_items/*`
- New: `bin/dev`, `Procfile.dev`, `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css` (via installer); shared `app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb`; the `*.turbo_stream.erb` templates; model + request specs.

## Verification
1. `bin/rails db:migrate` (ensure schema current), then `bin/dev` — opens with the Tailwind watcher.
2. Visit `http://localhost:3000/` and manually verify, watching that **no full page reloads** occur:
   - Add / edit / delete a todo list inline; clicking a list loads its items on the right.
   - Add / edit / delete a todo item inline; toggle a checkbox marks it complete (strikethrough); "Check all" completes every item.
3. `bin/rspec` is green.
4. Optionally drive the above with the Claude Preview MCP for screenshots.

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Hotwire + Tailwind Todo List App

## Context

The app is a Rails 7.0.4 Todo app (importmap-based) with `TodoList` and `TodoItem` models. Turbo + Stimulus are already installed, but the UI is unstyled scaffold output and every action does a full-page redirect. Tailwind is not installed, and `TodoList` is missing `show/edit/update/destroy` routes.

The goal: a single-page, two-pane app matching the user's mockup — **Todo Lists on the left, Todo Items (of the selected list) on the right** — where create/edit/delete/complete all happen inline without full page reloads, using **Turbo Frames + Turbo Streams** and styled with **Tailwind**.

Decisions confirmed with the user:
- Install Tailwind via the **`tailwindcss-rails` gem** (run app with `bin/dev`).
- **Inline** add/edit on the single page (Turbo Frames swap rows into forms).
- Include the **"Check all"** control (marks all items in a list complete).

## Implementation

### 1. Install Tailwind
- Add `gem "tailwindcss-rails"` to the `Gemfile`, `bundle install`, then run `bin/rails tailwindcss:install`.
- This generates `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css`, a `Procfile.dev`, `bin/dev`, and updates the layout to `<%= stylesheet_link_tag "tailwind", "data-turbo-track": "reload" %>`. Verify `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` picks up the Tailwind stylesheet.

### 2. Routes — `config/routes.rb`
Promote `todo_lists` to full CRUD and add a `check_all` collection route on items. Leave the `api` namespace untouched.
```ruby
resources :todo_lists, path: :todolists do
  resources :todo_items, path: :todoitems do
    patch :check_all, on: :collection
  end
end
root "todo_lists#index"
```

### 3. Controllers

**`app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`** — add `show`, `edit`, `update`, `destroy` and a `set_todo_list` before_action.
- `index`: `@todo_lists = TodoList.all`; set `@todo_list = @todo_lists.first` to seed the right pane, and `@new_todo_list = TodoList.new` for the add form.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: `respond_to` with `format.turbo_stream` (success) and `format.html` fallback; re-render the form partial with `status: :unprocessable_entity` on validation failure.

**`app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`** — add Turbo Stream responses and `check_all`.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: respond with `format.turbo_stream`.
- The per-item completion checkbox reuses `update` (submits `complete`).
- `check_all`: `@todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)` then re-render the items list via Turbo Stream.

### 4. Views — two-pane layout

**`todo_lists/index.html.erb`**: page header "Todo List App"; responsive 2-column grid (`grid md:grid-cols-2`). Left = lists pane; right = `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` rendering the selected list's items (or a "Select a list" placeholder when none).

**Left pane (lists):**
- `todo_lists/_todo_list.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_list)`; shows the name as a link that targets the `"todo_items_pane"` frame (`data: { turbo_frame: "todo_items_pane" }` → `todo_list_todo_items_path`), plus edit (pencil) and delete (✕) buttons. Container div `id="todo_lists"`.
- `todo_lists/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled, reused for the inline "Add todo list" form (persistent, `id="new_todo_list_form"`) and inline edit (rendered inside `edit.html.erb` wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(@todo_list)`).

**Right pane (items):**
- `todo_items/_pane.html.erb` (NEW shared partial): "Todo Items" header, "Check all" button (PATCH to `check_all_todo_list_todo_items_path`), inline "Add todo item" form, and a container `id="todo_items"` listing items.
- `todo_items/index.html.erb`: wraps `render "pane"` in `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` so list-row clicks lazy-load it; the lists index right pane renders the same partial — single source of truth.
- `todo_items/_todo_item.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_item)`; a completion checkbox in a mini `form_with` that auto-submits (`onchange: "this.form.requestSubmit()"`), the title (strikethrough when `complete`), edit + delete buttons.
- `todo_items/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled inline form (used for add + edit, like the lists form).

### 5. Turbo Stream templates
Add `*.turbo_stream.erb` for both controllers:
- Lists: `create` (prepend row to `#todo_lists` + reset `#new_todo_list_form`), `update` (replace `dom_id(list)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(list)`).
- Items: `create` (append to `#todo_items` + reset add-item form), `update` (replace `dom_id(item)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(item)`), `check_all` (replace `#todo_items` with the re-rendered list).

### 6. Tests — `spec/`
The current specs are mostly scaffold stubs; view specs reference markup being replaced and must be updated.
- Add model specs: `spec/models/todo_list_spec.rb`, `spec/models/todo_item_spec.rb` (validations, association, `complete` default).
- Fill in `spec/requests/todo_items_spec.rb` (real `valid_attributes`/`invalid_attributes`, assert turbo_stream responses for create/update/destroy + check_all). Add `spec/requests/todo_lists_spec.rb` covering the new actions.
- Update/replace the `spec/views/todo_items/*` specs to match the new partials, or convert to request-level coverage so the suite stays green.

## Files
- Modify: `Gemfile`, `config/routes.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`, `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb`, `app/views/todo_lists/*`, `app/views/todo_items/*`
- New: `bin/dev`, `Procfile.dev`, `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css` (via installer); shared `app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb`; the `*.turbo_stream.erb` templates; model + request specs.

## Verification
1. `bin/rails db:migrate` (ensure schema current), then `bin/dev` — opens with the Tailwind watcher.
2. Visit `http://localhost:3000/` and manually verify, watching that **no full page reloads** occur:
   - Add / edit / delete a todo list inline; clicking a list loads its items on the right.
   - Add / edit / delete a todo item inline; toggle a checkbox marks it complete (strikethrough); "Check all" completes every item.
3. `bin/rspec` is green.
4. Optionally drive the above with the Claude Preview MCP for screenshots.

# Bundle edge Rails instead: gem "rails", github: "rails/rails", branch: "main"
gem "rails", "~> 7.0.4", ">= 7.0.4.3"

# The original asset pipeline for Rails [https://github.com/rails/sprockets-rails]
gem "sprockets-rails"

# Use SQLite3 as the database for Active Record
gem "sqlite3", "~> 1.6"

# Use the Puma web server [https://github.com/puma/puma]
gem "puma", "~> 5.0"

# Use JavaScript with ESM import maps [https://github.com/rails/importmap-rails]
gem "importmap-rails"

# Hotwire's SPA-like page accelerator [https://turbo.hotwired.dev]
gem "turbo-rails"

# Hotwire's modest JavaScript framework [https://stimulus.hotwired.dev]
gem "stimulus-rails"

# Build JSON APIs with ease [https://github.com/rails/jbuilder]
gem "jbuilder"

# Use Redis adapter to run Action Cable in production
# gem "redis", "~> 4.0"

# Use Kredis to get higher-level data types in Redis [https://github.com/rails/kredis]
# gem "kredis"

# Use Active Model has_secure_password [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_model_basics.html#securepassword]
# gem "bcrypt", "~> 3.1.7"

# Windows does not include zoneinfo files, so bundle the tzinfo-data gem
gem "tzinfo-data", platforms: %i[ mingw mswin x64_mingw jruby ]

# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
gem "bootsnap", require: false

# Use Sass to process CSS
# gem "sassc-rails"

# Use Active Storage variants [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_storage_overview.html#transforming-images]
# gem "image_processing", "~> 1.2"

group :development, :test do
  # See https://guides.rubyonrails.org/debugging_rails_applications.html#debugging-with-the-debug-gem
  gem "debug", platforms: %i[ mri mingw x64_mingw ]
end

group :development do
  # Use console on exceptions pages [https://github.com/rails/web-console]
  gem "web-console"

  gem "rspec-rails"

  # Add speed badges [https://github.com/MiniProfiler/rack-mini-profiler]
  # gem "rack-mini-profiler"

  # Speed up commands on slow machines / big apps [https://github.com/rails/spring]
  # gem "spring"
end

group :test do
  # Use system testing [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/testing.html#system-testing]
  gem "rspec"
  gem "capybara"
  gem "selenium-webdriver"
  gem "webdrivers"
end

gem "rails", "~> 7.0.4", ">= 7.0.4.3"

# The original asset pipeline for Rails [https://github.com/rails/sprockets-rails]
gem "sprockets-rails"

# Use SQLite3 as the database for Active Record

# Use the Puma web server [https://github.com/puma/puma]
gem "puma", "~> 5.0"

gem "importmap-rails"

# Hotwire's SPA-like page accelerator [https://turbo.hotwired.dev]
gem "turbo-rails"

# Hotwire's modest JavaScript framework [https://stimulus.hotwired.dev]
gem "stimulus-rails"

# Use Tailwind CSS [https://github.com/rails/tailwindcss-rails]
gem "tailwindcss-rails"

# Build JSON APIs with ease [https://github.com/rails/jbuilder]

# Use Kredis to get higher-level data types in Redis [https://github.com/rails/kredis]

# Windows does not include zoneinfo files, so bundle the tzinfo-data gem
gem "tzinfo-data", platforms: %i[ mingw mswin x64_mingw jruby ]

# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
gem "bootsnap", require: false

# Use Sass to process CSS
# gem "sassc-rails"

# Use Active Storage variants [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_storage_overview.html#transforming-images]

end

  # Use system testing [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/testing.html#system-testing]

  gem "capybara"
  gem "selenium-webdriver"
  gem "webdrivers"
end

content.content:
---tailwind src---
@import "tailwindcss";
---builds---
total 16
drwxr-xr-x@ 5 rootstrap  staff   160 Jun 27 15:38 .
drwxr-xr-x@ 7 rootstrap  staff   224 Jun 27 15:38 ..
-rw-r--r--@ 1 rootstrap  staff     0 Jun 27 15:38 .keep
drwxr-xr-x@ 2 rootstrap  staff    64 Jun 27 15:38 tailwind
-rw-r--r--@ 1 rootstrap  staff  4864 Jun 27 15:38 tailwind.css
---bin/dev---
#!/usr/bin/env sh

class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all
    @todo_list = @todo_lists.first
    @new_todo_list = TodoList.new
  end

  # GET /todolists/1
  def show
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new
  end

  # GET /todolists/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)

    if @todo_list.save
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully created.' }
      end
    else
      @new_todo_list = @todo_list
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todolists/1
  def update
    if @todo_list.update(todo_list_params)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully updated.' }
      end
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/1
  def destroy
    @todo_list.destroy!

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully destroyed.' }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list
  before_action :set_todo_item, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def index
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def show
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully created.' }
      end
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def update
    if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully updated.' }
      end
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def destroy
    @todo_item.destroy!

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully destroyed.' }
    end
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/check_all
  def check_all
    @todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list) }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def set_todo_item
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:title, :complete)
  end
end

class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list
  before_action :set_todo_item, only: %i[ show edit update destroy ]

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items
  def index
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def show
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items 
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully created.'
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def update
    if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully updated.'
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todo_lists/:todo_list_id/todo_items/1
  def destroy
    @todo_item.destroy!
    redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully destroyed.'
  end

  private
    # Use callbacks to share common setup or constraints between actions.
    def set_todo_list
      @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
    end

    # Use callbacks to share common setup or constraints between actions.
    def set_todo_item
      @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
    end

    # Only allow a list of trusted parameters through.
    def todo_item_params
      params.require(:todo_item).permit(:title, :complete)
    end
end

class TodoListsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  # GET /todolists
  def index
    @todo_lists = TodoList.all
    @todo_list = @todo_lists.first
    @new_todo_list = TodoList.new
  end

  # GET /todolists/1
  def show
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  # GET /todolists/new
  def new
    @todo_list = TodoList.new
  end

  # GET /todolists/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todolists
  def create
    @todo_list = TodoList.new(todo_list_params)

    if @todo_list.save
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully created.' }
      end
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todolists/1
  def update
    if @todo_list.update(todo_list_params)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully updated.' }
      end
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/1
  def destroy
    @todo_list.destroy!

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_lists_path, notice: 'Todo list was successfully destroyed.' }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:id])
  end

  def todo_list_params
    params.require(:todo_list).permit(:name)
  end
end

class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list
  before_action :set_todo_item, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def index
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def show
    redirect_to todo_lists_path
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully created.' }
      end
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def update
    if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully updated.' }
      end
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def destroy
    @todo_item.destroy!

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully destroyed.' }
    end
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/check_all
  def check_all
    @todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list) }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def set_todo_item
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:title, :complete)
  end
end

class TodoItemsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_todo_list
  before_action :set_todo_item, only: %i[show edit update destroy]

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def index
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def show
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/new
  def new
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build
  end

  # GET /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1/edit
  def edit
  end

  # POST /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems
  def create
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build(todo_item_params)

    if @todo_item.save
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully created.' }
      end
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # PATCH/PUT /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def update
    if @todo_item.update(todo_item_params)
      respond_to do |format|
        format.turbo_stream
        format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully updated.' }
      end
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  # DELETE /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/1
  def destroy
    @todo_item.destroy!

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list), notice: 'Todo item was successfully destroyed.' }
    end
  end

  # PATCH /todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems/check_all
  def check_all
    @todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)
    @todo_items = @todo_list.todo_items
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.build

    respond_to do |format|
      format.turbo_stream
      format.html { redirect_to todo_list_todo_items_path(@todo_list) }
    end
  end

  private

  def set_todo_list
    @todo_list = TodoList.find(params[:todo_list_id])
  end

  def set_todo_item
    @todo_item = @todo_list.todo_items.find(params[:id])
  end

  def todo_item_params
    params.require(:todo_item).permit(:title, :complete)
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

# This spec was generated by rspec-rails when you ran the scaffold generator.
# It demonstrates how one might use RSpec to test the controller code that
# was generated by Rails when you ran the scaffold generator.
#
# It assumes that the implementation code is generated by the rails scaffold
# generator. If you are using any extension libraries to generate different
# controller code, this generated spec may or may not pass.
#
# It only uses APIs available in rails and/or rspec-rails. There are a number
# of tools you can use to make these specs even more expressive, but we're
# sticking to rails and rspec-rails APIs to keep things simple and stable.

RSpec.describe "/todo_items", type: :request do
  
  # This should return the minimal set of attributes required to create a valid
  # TodoItem. As you add validations to TodoItem, be sure to
  # adjust the attributes here as well.
  let(:valid_attributes) {
    skip("Add a hash of attributes valid for your model")
  }

  let(:invalid_attributes) {
    skip("Add a hash of attributes invalid for your model")
  }

  describe "GET /index" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
      get todo_items_url
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "GET /show" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
      get todo_item_url(todo_item)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "GET /new" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      get new_todo_item_url
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "GET /edit" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
      get edit_todo_item_url(todo_item)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "POST /create" do
    context "with valid parameters" do
      it "creates a new TodoItem" do
        expect {
          post todo_items_url, params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }
        }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(1)
      end

      it "redirects to the created todo_item" do
        post todo_items_url, params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }
        expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_item_url(TodoItem.last))
      end
    end

    context "with invalid parameters" do
      it "does not create a new TodoItem" do
        expect {
          post todo_items_url, params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(0)
      end

    
      it "renders a response with 422 status (i.e. to display the 'new' template)" do
        post todo_items_url, params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
      end
    
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /update" do
    context "with valid parameters" do
      let(:new_attributes) {
        skip("Add a hash of attributes valid for your model")
      }

      it "updates the requested todo_item" do
        todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
        patch todo_item_url(todo_item), params: { todo_item: new_attributes }
        todo_item.reload
        skip("Add assertions for updated state")
      end

      it "redirects to the todo_item" do
        todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
        patch todo_item_url(todo_item), params: { todo_item: new_attributes }
        todo_item.reload
        expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_item_url(todo_item))
      end
    end

    context "with invalid parameters" do
    
      it "renders a response with 422 status (i.e. to display the 'edit' template)" do
        todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
        patch todo_item_url(todo_item), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
      end
    
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /destroy" do
    it "destroys the requested todo_item" do
      todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
      expect {
        delete todo_item_url(todo_item)
      }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(-1)
    end

    it "redirects to the todo_items list" do
      todo_item = TodoItem.create! valid_attributes
      delete todo_item_url(todo_item)
      expect(response).to redirect_to(todo_items_url)
    end
  end
end

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe "/todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems", type: :request do
  let(:todo_list) { TodoList.create!(name: "Groceries") }

  let(:valid_attributes) { { title: "Buy milk" } }
  let(:invalid_attributes) { { title: "" } }

  let(:turbo_headers) { { "Accept" => "text/vnd.turbo-stream.html" } }

  describe "GET /index" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      get todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "GET /edit" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      get edit_todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "POST /create" do
    context "with valid parameters" do
      it "creates a new TodoItem scoped to the list" do
        expect {
          post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }
        }.to change(todo_list.todo_items, :count).by(1)
      end

      it "responds with a turbo stream" do
        post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list),
             params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }, headers: turbo_headers
        expect(response.media_type).to eq Mime[:turbo_stream]
        expect(response.body).to include("turbo-stream")
      end
    end

    context "with invalid parameters" do
      it "does not create a new TodoItem" do
        expect {
          post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(0)
      end

      it "responds with 422" do
        post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
      end
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /update" do
    it "updates the requested todo_item" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      patch todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item),
            params: { todo_item: { complete: true } }, headers: turbo_headers
      expect(todo_item.reload.complete).to be true
      expect(response.media_type).to eq Mime[:turbo_stream]
    end

    it "responds with 422 for invalid params" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      patch todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /destroy" do
    it "destroys the requested todo_item" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      expect {
        delete todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item), headers: turbo_headers
      }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(-1)
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /check_all" do
    it "marks every item in the list complete" do
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(title: "a", complete: false)
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(title: "b", complete: false)
      patch check_all_todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), headers: turbo_headers
      expect(todo_list.todo_items.pluck(:complete)).to all(be true)

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe "/todolists/:todo_list_id/todoitems", type: :request do
  let(:todo_list) { TodoList.create!(name: "Groceries") }

  let(:valid_attributes) { { title: "Buy milk" } }
  let(:invalid_attributes) { { title: "" } }

  let(:turbo_headers) { { "Accept" => "text/vnd.turbo-stream.html" } }

  describe "GET /index" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      get todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "GET /edit" do
    it "renders a successful response" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      get edit_todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item)
      expect(response).to be_successful
    end
  end

  describe "POST /create" do
    context "with valid parameters" do
      it "creates a new TodoItem scoped to the list" do
        expect {
          post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }
        }.to change(todo_list.todo_items, :count).by(1)
      end

      it "responds with a turbo stream" do
        post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list),
             params: { todo_item: valid_attributes }, headers: turbo_headers
        expect(response.media_type).to eq Mime[:turbo_stream]
        expect(response.body).to include("turbo-stream")
      end
    end

    context "with invalid parameters" do
      it "does not create a new TodoItem" do
        expect {
          post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(0)
      end

      it "responds with 422" do
        post todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
        expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
      end
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /update" do
    it "updates the requested todo_item" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      patch todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item),
            params: { todo_item: { complete: true } }, headers: turbo_headers
      expect(todo_item.reload.complete).to be true
      expect(response.media_type).to eq Mime[:turbo_stream]
    end

    it "responds with 422 for invalid params" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      patch todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item), params: { todo_item: invalid_attributes }
      expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  describe "DELETE /destroy" do
    it "destroys the requested todo_item" do
      todo_item = todo_list.todo_items.create!(valid_attributes)
      expect {
        delete todo_list_todo_item_url(todo_list, todo_item), headers: turbo_headers
      }.to change(TodoItem, :count).by(-1)
    end
  end

  describe "PATCH /check_all" do
    it "marks every item in the list complete" do
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(title: "a", complete: false)
      todo_list.todo_items.create!(title: "b", complete: false)
      patch check_all_todo_list_todo_items_url(todo_list), headers: turbo_headers
      expect(todo_list.todo_items.pluck(:complete)).to all(be true)
      expect(response.media_type).to eq Mime[:turbo_stream]
    end
  end
end

input.content:
require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe TodoItem, type: :model do
  let(:todo_list) { TodoList.create!(name: "Groceries") }

  it "is valid with a title and a list" do
    expect(todo_list.todo_items.build(title: "Milk")).to be_valid
  end

  it "requires a title" do
    item = todo_list.todo_items.build(title: "")
    expect(item).not_to be_valid
    expect(item.errors[:title]).to be_present
  end

  it "requires a todo_list" do
    expect(TodoItem.new(title: "Milk")).not_to be_valid
  end

  it "defaults complete to false" do
    expect(todo_list.todo_items.create!(title: "Milk").complete).to be false

content.text:
Done. Here's what I built.

## Summary

A single-page, two-pane Todo app matching your mockup — **Todo Lists on the left, Todo Items on the right** — where add/edit/delete/complete all happen inline via **Turbo Frames + Turbo Streams**, styled with **Tailwind**. No full page reloads.

### What changed

**Tailwind** — added `tailwindcss-rails`, generated [application.css](app/assets/tailwind/application.css), `Procfile.dev`, and `bin/dev`. The layout ([application.html.erb](app/views/layouts/application.html.erb)) now loads the Tailwind build.

**Routes** ([config/routes.rb](config/routes.rb)) — promoted `todo_lists` to full CRUD, added a `check_all` collection route on items, and a `root`.

**Controllers** — [TodoListsController](app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb) gained `show/edit/update/destroy`; both controllers now respond with `turbo_stream` for create/update/destroy, and [TodoItemsController](app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb) got `check_all`.

**Views** — rebuilt into the two-pane layout:
- Left: [index.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb) with an inline add form and clickable list rows ([_todo_list](app/views/todo_lists/_todo_list.html.erb)) that load items into the right frame; edit swaps the row into an inline form.
- Right: shared [_pane.html.erb](app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb) with "Check all", an add-item form, and rows ([_todo_item](app/views/todo_items/_todo_item.html.erb)) with an auto-submitting completion checkbox (strikethrough when done) and edit/delete.
- Plus `*.turbo_stream.erb` templates for both resources.

**Tests** — rewrote routing/request specs for the nested routes + Turbo Stream responses, added model specs, removed the obsolete scaffold view specs. **37 examples, 0 failures.**

### One pre-existing issue I had to fix
The app **didn't boot at all** on this machine's Ruby 4.0.2 — old Rails 7.0 expects stdlib gems (`logger`, `base64`, `cgi`, etc.) that newer Ruby no longer auto-loads. I declared them in the [Gemfile](Gemfile) so the app, tests, and server run. This was unrelated to the feature but blocked everything.

### Verified
- `bin/rspec` → 37/37 green
- Turbo Stream responses for create/update/delete/check_all confirmed via live requests
- Visual layout confirmed (screenshots above) at both narrow (stacks) and wide (side-by-side) widths

### How to run
```
bin/dev      # runs Rails + the Tailwind watcher
```
Then open http://localhost:3000. (README updated with this.)

One small known trade-off: editing or deleting the *currently-selected* list won't auto-refresh the right pane's selection highlight until the next click — kept simple since selection isn't tracked server-side. Easy to add if you'd like it.

content.text:
Both row containers already use `space-y-3` from my last change — but your screenshot still shows the tight `space-y-2` spacing. That's the tell: **the compiled Tailwind CSS wasn't regenerated**, so the `space-y-3` utility isn't in the stylesheet yet and has no effect. This happens if the app is served without the Tailwind watcher.

Here's my plan.

input.content:
# Increase vertical spacing between todo rows

## Context

The user wants more visible vertical space between the rows in both panes — between each todo *list* row on the left, and between each todo *item* row on the right (per the marks drawn in the attached screenshot).

The row containers were already bumped from `space-y-2` to `space-y-3` in a prior turn, but the user's screenshot still shows the tighter spacing. The reason is that the compiled Tailwind stylesheet (`app/assets/builds/tailwind.css`) was **not regenerated** after that edit — the dev server was stopped, so no watcher ran and the `space-y-3` utility never made it into the CSS. Whenever a new Tailwind class is introduced, the stylesheet must be rebuilt (the `bin/dev` watcher does this automatically; `bin/puma` alone does not).

## Implementation

1. Increase the row gap to a clearly visible value (`space-y-4` = 1rem) in both containers:
   - [app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_lists" class="mt-4 space-y-3">` → `space-y-4`
   - [app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb](app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_items" class="space-y-3">` → `space-y-4`

2. Regenerate the Tailwind build so the new utility is included:
   - `bin/rails tailwindcss:build`
   - (Or simply run the app via `bin/dev`, whose `tailwindcss:watch` process rebuilds on every change.)

## Verification

2. Regenerate the Tailwind build so the new utility is included:
   - `bin/rails tailwindcss:build`
   - (Or simply run the app via `bin/dev`, whose `tailwindcss:watch` process rebuilds on every change.)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Hotwire + Tailwind Todo List App

## Context

The app is a Rails 7.0.4 Todo app (importmap-based) with `TodoList` and `TodoItem` models. Turbo + Stimulus are already installed, but the UI is unstyled scaffold output and every action does a full-page redirect. Tailwind is not installed, and `TodoList` is missing `show/edit/update/destroy` routes.

The goal: a single-page, two-pane app matching the user's mockup — **Todo Lists on the left, Todo Items (of the selected list) on the right** — where create/edit/delete/complete all happen inline without full page reloads, using **Turbo Frames + Turbo Streams** and styled with **Tailwind**.

Decisions confirmed with the user:
- Install Tailwind via the **`tailwindcss-rails` gem** (run app with `bin/dev`).
- **Inline** add/edit on the single page (Turbo Frames swap rows into forms).
- Include the **"Check all"** control (marks all items in a list complete).

## Implementation

### 1. Install Tailwind
- Add `gem "tailwindcss-rails"` to the `Gemfile`, `bundle install`, then run `bin/rails tailwindcss:install`.
- This generates `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css`, a `Procfile.dev`, `bin/dev`, and updates the layout to `<%= stylesheet_link_tag "tailwind", "data-turbo-track": "reload" %>`. Verify `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` picks up the Tailwind stylesheet.

### 2. Routes — `config/routes.rb`
Promote `todo_lists` to full CRUD and add a `check_all` collection route on items. Leave the `api` namespace untouched.
```ruby
resources :todo_lists, path: :todolists do
  resources :todo_items, path: :todoitems do
    patch :check_all, on: :collection
  end
end
root "todo_lists#index"
```

### 3. Controllers

**`app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`** — add `show`, `edit`, `update`, `destroy` and a `set_todo_list` before_action.
- `index`: `@todo_lists = TodoList.all`; set `@todo_list = @todo_lists.first` to seed the right pane, and `@new_todo_list = TodoList.new` for the add form.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: `respond_to` with `format.turbo_stream` (success) and `format.html` fallback; re-render the form partial with `status: :unprocessable_entity` on validation failure.

**`app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`** — add Turbo Stream responses and `check_all`.
- `create` / `update` / `destroy`: respond with `format.turbo_stream`.
- The per-item completion checkbox reuses `update` (submits `complete`).
- `check_all`: `@todo_list.todo_items.update_all(complete: true)` then re-render the items list via Turbo Stream.

### 4. Views — two-pane layout

**`todo_lists/index.html.erb`**: page header "Todo List App"; responsive 2-column grid (`grid md:grid-cols-2`). Left = lists pane; right = `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` rendering the selected list's items (or a "Select a list" placeholder when none).

**Left pane (lists):**
- `todo_lists/_todo_list.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_list)`; shows the name as a link that targets the `"todo_items_pane"` frame (`data: { turbo_frame: "todo_items_pane" }` → `todo_list_todo_items_path`), plus edit (pencil) and delete (✕) buttons. Container div `id="todo_lists"`.
- `todo_lists/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled, reused for the inline "Add todo list" form (persistent, `id="new_todo_list_form"`) and inline edit (rendered inside `edit.html.erb` wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(@todo_list)`).

**Right pane (items):**
- `todo_items/_pane.html.erb` (NEW shared partial): "Todo Items" header, "Check all" button (PATCH to `check_all_todo_list_todo_items_path`), inline "Add todo item" form, and a container `id="todo_items"` listing items.
- `todo_items/index.html.erb`: wraps `render "pane"` in `turbo_frame_tag "todo_items_pane"` so list-row clicks lazy-load it; the lists index right pane renders the same partial — single source of truth.
- `todo_items/_todo_item.html.erb`: row wrapped in `turbo_frame_tag dom_id(todo_item)`; a completion checkbox in a mini `form_with` that auto-submits (`onchange: "this.form.requestSubmit()"`), the title (strikethrough when `complete`), edit + delete buttons.
- `todo_items/_form.html.erb`: Tailwind-styled inline form (used for add + edit, like the lists form).

### 5. Turbo Stream templates
Add `*.turbo_stream.erb` for both controllers:
- Lists: `create` (prepend row to `#todo_lists` + reset `#new_todo_list_form`), `update` (replace `dom_id(list)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(list)`).
- Items: `create` (append to `#todo_items` + reset add-item form), `update` (replace `dom_id(item)`), `destroy` (remove `dom_id(item)`), `check_all` (replace `#todo_items` with the re-rendered list).

### 6. Tests — `spec/`
The current specs are mostly scaffold stubs; view specs reference markup being replaced and must be updated.
- Add model specs: `spec/models/todo_list_spec.rb`, `spec/models/todo_item_spec.rb` (validations, association, `complete` default).
- Fill in `spec/requests/todo_items_spec.rb` (real `valid_attributes`/`invalid_attributes`, assert turbo_stream responses for create/update/destroy + check_all). Add `spec/requests/todo_lists_spec.rb` covering the new actions.
- Update/replace the `spec/views/todo_items/*` specs to match the new partials, or convert to request-level coverage so the suite stays green.

## Files
- Modify: `Gemfile`, `config/routes.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_lists_controller.rb`, `app/controllers/todo_items_controller.rb`, `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb`, `app/views/todo_lists/*`, `app/views/todo_items/*`
- New: `bin/dev`, `Procfile.dev`, `app/assets/stylesheets/application.tailwind.css` (via installer); shared `app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb`; the `*.turbo_stream.erb` templates; model + request specs.

## Verification
1. `bin/rails db:migrate` (ensure schema current), then `bin/dev` — opens with the Tailwind watcher.
2. Visit `http://localhost:3000/` and manually verify, watching that **no full page reloads** occur:
   - Add / edit / delete a todo list inline; clicking a list loads its items on the right.
   - Add / edit / delete a todo item inline; toggle a checkbox marks it complete (strikethrough); "Check all" completes every item.
3. `bin/rspec` is green.
4. Optionally drive the above with the Claude Preview MCP for screenshots.

content.text:
That changes the diagnosis. If `space-y-3` made **no** difference at all, the spacing utility is being ignored — and the reason is a classic Turbo gotcha: each row is a `<turbo-frame>` element, and custom elements default to `display: inline`. Tailwind's `space-y-*` works by setting `margin-top` on child elements, but **margins are ignored on inline elements** — so no value of `space-y` will ever move them.

The fix is to make the container a flex column with `gap`, which blockifies its children so the gap actually applies (works regardless of the inline frames). Let me update the plan.

input.content:
# Fix vertical spacing between todo rows

## Context

The user wants a clearly visible vertical gap between rows in both panes (list rows on the left, item rows on the right). Increasing `space-y-*` on the containers had **no effect** — the real cause: each row is wrapped in a `<turbo-frame>` custom element, which defaults to `display: inline`. Tailwind's `space-y-*` utility adds `margin-top` to children, and **margins are ignored on inline elements**, so no `space-y` value ever changes the spacing.

Fix: switch the containers from `space-y-*` to a **flex column with `gap`** (`flex flex-col gap-4`). Flex blockifies its children, so the gap applies to the turbo-frame rows. The app runs via `bin/dev`, so the Tailwind watcher recompiles automatically.

## Implementation

Replace the row-container classes:
- [app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_lists" class="mt-4 space-y-3">` → `class="mt-4 flex flex-col gap-4"`
- [app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb](app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_items" class="space-y-3">` → `class="flex flex-col gap-4"`

(Alternative if preferred later: add `turbo-frame { display: block }` globally, then `space-y-*` would also work. The flex+gap approach is the minimal, self-contained fix.)

## Verification

- With `bin/dev` running, reload http://localhost:3000 and confirm an even, visible gap now separates each list row and each item row.
- Tune the single `gap-*` value to taste (`gap-3` tighter, `gap-6` looser).

aiTitle: Add Hotwire and Tailwind to todo list app

content.text:
Good catch. The cause: the "selected" highlight is decided server-side on initial load (first list = highlighted). When you click another list, only the **right pane** navigates via Turbo Frame — the left list rows aren't re-rendered, so the highlight never moves off Groceries. This is the selection trade-off I'd flagged earlier.

The clean Hotwire fix is a tiny Stimulus controller that moves the highlight on click (no server round-trip). Let me look at the current row markup and wire it up.

content.text: Confirmed working — clicking **Weekend** moved the highlight to it (Groceries and Work both deselected) and loaded its items on the right. The selection now follows your click. Let me stop the verification server.
Server 1014b429-b862-4689-b30e-aea2d0106542 stopped

Fixed. Reload your `bin/dev` tab and the selection highlight will now follow whichever list you click.

**What was happening:** the highlight was computed server-side only on the initial page load (first list = highlighted). Clicking a list only navigates the right-hand Turbo Frame, so the left rows never re-rendered and the highlight stayed stuck on Groceries.

**The fix** — a small Stimulus controller, [selection_controller.js](app/javascript/controllers/selection_controller.js):
- [index.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb) — `#todo_lists` container gets `data-controller="selection"`.
- [_todo_list.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/_todo_list.html.erb) — each row is a `data-selection-target="row"` and its name link fires `click->selection#select`.
- On click it clears the highlight classes from every row and applies them to the clicked one — purely client-side, no extra request.

It reuses the same Tailwind classes already in the build, so no CSS rebuild was needed; it loads automatically via importmap. Verified end-to-end in a browser (clicked Work, then Weekend — highlight tracked correctly each time).

I also added a `verify` config to [.claude/launch.json](.claude/launch.json) (a watcher-free server on an auto-assigned port) so I could test without disturbing your `bin/dev` on 3000 — harmless to keep, or I can remove it.

content.text: Fixed — after the validation error, the "Add todo list" input now spans the full column width with the **+** button right after it (matching the normal state), instead of shrinking. The same fix covers the item form since both use turbo-frames. Let me stop the verification server.
Server 6824003b-55cb-4462-ac8e-0339fea886f4 stopped

Done. The form no longer shrinks when an error appears.

**Cause:** `<turbo-frame>` is a custom element that defaults to `display: inline`. On a full page load the form rendered full-width, but when Turbo swapped the frame's contents for the 422 error response, the inline frame shrank to fit its content — so the input narrowed.

**Fix:** one global rule in [application.css](app/assets/tailwind/application.css) making all frames block-level:
```css
turbo-frame { display: block; }
```
This also hardens the earlier row-spacing behavior and any future frame layout. Verified in a browser: submitted an empty list form, and the input stayed full-width with the error banner above it.

Your `bin/dev` watcher will rebuild the CSS automatically (it's a source change) — just reload the page.

Sorted, and this time it'll actually reach your browser. Just **reload** http://localhost:3000 (no need to restart `bin/dev`).

**Why my first attempt didn't work for you:** I put the rule in the Tailwind *source* (`app/assets/tailwind/application.css`). The `tailwindcss:watch` process in `bin/dev` watches your templates for utility-class usage — it doesn't reliably rebuild when the source CSS file itself changes, so your running app kept serving the old stylesheet.

**The fix now** lives in the sprockets stylesheet, [app/assets/stylesheets/application.css](app/assets/stylesheets/application.css):
```css
turbo-frame { display: block; }
```
Sprockets recompiles this on any reload (independent of Tailwind), and the `data-turbo-track: "reload"` on the stylesheet tag forces the page to pick up the new digest. I reverted the Tailwind-source edit so there's a single source of truth.

I verified it the rigorous way: with the rule present **only** in `application.css`, after triggering the validation error both frames compute `display: block`, the form stays full-width (305px), and the rule loads from `application-*.css` — plus the screenshot above shows the error state at full width.

The rule **is** being served correctly — your `application.css` contains `turbo-frame { display: block; }` and `tailwind.css` no longer does. So the server side is right; the problem is that your browser isn't applying it — almost certainly a **cached page / stylesheet** from before the fix (a Turbo frame submit doesn't re-fetch CSS, and a soft reload can serve the cached file).

Let me read the layout and form markup to confirm there's nothing else at play and to choose the most cache-proof fix.

<html>
  <head>
    <title>Todo List App</title>
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1">
    <%= csrf_meta_tags %>
    <%= csp_meta_tag %>

      <%= yield %>
    </main>

content.text:
I've confirmed the server serves the rule correctly (`application.css` has `turbo-frame { display: block }`, `tailwind.css` doesn't). So the code fix is right — the problem is your browser is rendering a **stale page**: a Turbo frame submit never re-fetches CSS, and Turbo Drive keeps a page snapshot, so a soft reload can show pre-fix HTML/CSS. That's why both prior fixes verified for me but not for you.

I'll make the rule cache-proof by shipping it inline in the HTML `<head>` (so it arrives with every server-rendered page, not as a separately-cached asset), and the verification will first reproduce the broken state to prove the lever is correct. Here's the plan.

input.content:
# Make the turbo-frame display fix actually reach the browser

## Context

When a create form fails validation, the "Add todo list" / "Add todo item" inputs shrink instead of filling their column. Root cause (already diagnosed): `<turbo-frame>` is a custom element that defaults to `display: inline`, so when Turbo replaces the frame's contents with the 422 error response, the frame shrink-wraps its content.

The fix — forcing `turbo-frame { display: block }` — is correct and is **already being served** (confirmed by curling the live server: the rule is in `application.css`, and `tailwind.css` no longer has it). But the user still sees the bug because the browser is showing a **stale page**: a Turbo Frame form submit does not re-fetch stylesheets, and Turbo Drive caches page snapshots, so a soft reload can keep the pre-fix HTML/CSS. Prior fixes therefore verified in a clean browser but not in the user's cached session.

Goal: deliver the rule in a way that can't be served stale as a separate asset, and verify it against a real reproduction of the broken state.

## Implementation

1. Move the rule from the separately-cached stylesheet into an inline `<style>` in the document `<head>`, so it ships with every server-rendered HTML response:
   - [app/views/layouts/application.html.erb](app/views/layouts/application.html.erb) — add inside `<head>`:
     ```erb
     <style>turbo-frame { display: block; }</style>
     ```
   - [app/assets/stylesheets/application.css](app/assets/stylesheets/application.css) — remove the now-redundant `turbo-frame { display: block; }` block (single source of truth).

2. No Tailwind rebuild needed (rule is plain CSS in the layout).

## Verification

Use a fresh `verify` preview server (port auto-assigned; the user's `bin/dev` stays on 3000):
1. **Reproduce the bug first** to prove the lever: temporarily confirm that without the rule the add-form frame computes `display: inline` and the input is narrow after a failed submit. (Inspect computed `display` + `getBoundingClientRect().width` via `preview_eval`.)
2. Apply the inline `<style>` fix, reload, submit an empty "Add todo list" form and an empty "Add todo item" form.
3. Confirm for BOTH forms in the error state: frame `display` is `block`, and the input row spans the full column width (input width ≈ frame width), with the error banner above it.
4. Screenshot the error state to visually confirm full-width forms.

## User guidance

Because the earlier fixes were masked by caching, instruct the user to do a **hard reload** (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R) once. With the inline style this is only needed once; future loads ship the rule in the HTML.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fix vertical spacing between todo rows

## Context

The user wants a clearly visible vertical gap between rows in both panes (list rows on the left, item rows on the right). Increasing `space-y-*` on the containers had **no effect** — the real cause: each row is wrapped in a `<turbo-frame>` custom element, which defaults to `display: inline`. Tailwind's `space-y-*` utility adds `margin-top` to children, and **margins are ignored on inline elements**, so no `space-y` value ever changes the spacing.

Fix: switch the containers from `space-y-*` to a **flex column with `gap`** (`flex flex-col gap-4`). Flex blockifies its children, so the gap applies to the turbo-frame rows. The app runs via `bin/dev`, so the Tailwind watcher recompiles automatically.

## Implementation

Replace the row-container classes:
- [app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb](app/views/todo_lists/index.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_lists" class="mt-4 space-y-3">` → `class="mt-4 flex flex-col gap-4"`
- [app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb](app/views/todo_items/_pane.html.erb) — `<div id="todo_items" class="space-y-3">` → `class="flex flex-col gap-4"`

(Alternative if preferred later: add `turbo-frame { display: block }` globally, then `space-y-*` would also work. The flex+gap approach is the minimal, self-contained fix.)

## Verification

- With `bin/dev` running, reload http://localhost:3000 and confirm an even, visible gap now separates each list row and each item row.
- Tune the single `gap-*` value to taste (`gap-3` tighter, `gap-6` looser).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * This is a manifest file that'll be compiled into application.css, which will include all the files
 * listed below.
 *
 * Any CSS (and SCSS, if configured) file within this directory, lib/assets/stylesheets, or any plugin's
 * vendor/assets/stylesheets directory can be referenced here using a relative path.
 *
 * You're free to add application-wide styles to this file and they'll appear at the bottom of the
 * compiled file so the styles you add here take precedence over styles defined in any other CSS
 * files in this directory. Styles in this file should be added after the last require_* statement.
 * It is generally better to create a new file per style scope.
 *
 *= require_tree .
 *= require_self
 */

/* Custom elements default to display: inline, which makes Turbo Frames shrink to
   fit their content (e.g. the add forms collapse when re-rendered with validation
   errors). Force block-level so frames always fill their container.
   Kept in the sprockets stylesheet (not the Tailwind source) so it recompiles on
   reload without depending on the Tailwind watcher. */
turbo-frame {
  display: block;
}

lines: -/* Custom elements default to display: inline, which makes Turbo Frames shrink to
-   fit their content (e.g. the add forms collapse when re-rendered with validation
-   errors). Force block-level so frames always fill their container.

content.text:
Found the **real** root cause — and it's not the turbo-frame after all. The server response shows that on a validation error, Rails wraps the errored input in `
`: ```html
← wrapper breaks the flex ``` That wrapper div becomes the flex child instead of the input, so the input's `flex-1` no longer applies — it collapses to its default width. That's why it only shrinks on error, and why my turbo-frame fix never helped (my earlier "verifications" measured the page *before* the async error frame finished swapping in — a false positive). The standard Rails fix is to stop wrapping errored fields. Let me add an initializer.

input.content:
# By default Rails wraps form fields that have validation errors in
# <div class="field_with_errors">. That extra block-level wrapper breaks our
# flex layouts (e.g. the add-todo input loses its `flex-1` and collapses to its
# default width when the form re-renders with errors). Render the field inline
# instead, leaving error messaging to the explicit error banner in the form.
Rails.application.config.action_view.field_error_proc = proc { |html_tag, _instance| html_tag.html_safe }

content.text:
Fixed for real this time, and verified in the actual error state. **37 examples, 0 failures.**

**The real cause** (my earlier diagnosis was wrong — sorry for the runaround): when a form fails validation, Rails wraps the errored input in `<div class="field_with_errors">`. That wrapper became the flex child instead of the `<input>`, so the input's `flex-1` stopped applying and it collapsed to its default width. The turbo-frame theory was a red herring — my previous "verifications" measured the page *before* the async error frame finished loading, so I never actually saw the broken state.

**The fix** — [config/initializers/field_error_proc.rb](config/initializers/field_error_proc.rb):
```ruby
Rails.application.config.action_view.field_error_proc = proc { |html_tag, _instance| html_tag.html_safe }
```
This stops Rails from adding the wrapper div, so the input stays a direct flex child and keeps its full width. Error messaging still shows via the explicit red banner in the form. I also removed the unnecessary `turbo-frame` CSS rule I'd added earlier, since it wasn't the cause.

Verified on a fresh server in the genuine error state: both forms show the error banner with the input at full column width (list input 565px in a 614px frame; item input 471px), and there's no `field_with_errors` wrapper in the DOM.

**Important:** this is an initializer, so it only loads at boot — **restart your `bin/dev`** (Ctrl+C and run it again), then submit an empty form to confirm. A reload alone won't pick it up.
